name: Pon Pla Tu Mackerel Salad
intro: This recipe, when fi nished, looks a lot like tuna salad. However, it bears all the hallmarks of a good

ingredients.item: Thai garlic cloves (preferably as they have better fl avor) or 3–4 regular cloves garlic
Thai shallots (preferably as they have better fl avor) or 4 regular shallots
fi nger-length green chilies (prik chee fah, if available)
small bird’s eye chilies (deseeded if concerned about spiciness)
fi sh sauce (if using a salty fi sh like herring, adjust down to taste)

method: Roast in a skillet or oven the garlic, shallots with skins on, and both types of chil- ies. Please make sure to prick the chilies with a fork or they will explode. Roast until blackened. When done, peel the green chilies and cut into thirds.
With a mortar and pestle, pound the garlic and shallots (remove the skins) into pieces, and add the chilies gradually.
Add the fi sh sauce and lime juice, and mix gently.
Add the shredded fi sh into the dip with a spoon and combine. Taste again and add another squirt of lime juice if the mix is not bright enough.
Serve garnished with the cori- ander leaves and accompanied by
1 small purple eggplant (preferable Asian) sliced into 1-inch (2.5-cm) pieces and cut into ½ inch (1.25 cm) squares
Heat a pan over medium-high heat. When the pan is hot, add the unscented oil.
Break the egg into a bowl and beat with a fork. Season with the salt and pepper.
Take the eggplant squares and dip them into the egg briefl y before frying them in the hot pan until cooked through. Con- tinue until all pieces are fried. Set aside.
Mix the pork, salt, pepper, garlic and egg together.
Form into small pork pat- ties like tiny sliders. Fry in a pan with hot unscented oil until cooked through.
